_comment: Expected comparison output. Arm A = namespace-only isolation (victim+aggressor on same node). Arm B = node isolation (separate nodes). II_B measures how much better node isolation is. On a t3.micro single-node, Arm B cannot run — this represents ideal 2-node results.

arm_A_namespace_isolation.description: Victim and aggressor share the same node; namespace ResourceQuota only

arm_B_node_isolation.description: Victim on dedicated node; aggressor on separate node

_interpretation.ii_comparison: Arm B P95 is 14.8% LOWER than Arm A, confirming node isolation provides stronger latency guarantees.
_interpretation.cost_tradeoff: Node isolation requires 2x the node count per tenant — doubles infrastructure cost.
_interpretation.conclusion: Namespace isolation II=0.20; Node isolation II≈0 (aggressor physically separated). Namespace isolation is acceptable for trusted tenants; node isolation is required for SLA-bound or hostile tenants.
